text: The surgeon opens the anterior mediastinal pleura and dissects the superior pulmonary vein, isolating the middle lobe vein. The fissure is dissected, followed by the lymph node compressing the middle lobe bronchus. Using conventional Watts instruments, the middle lobe bronchus is encircled and dissected. The middle lobe artery is encircled, hemoclips are placed, and the artery is divided with scissors.
The surgeon completes the lobectomy procedure by firing multiple stapler devices to resect the middle lobe bronchus.
